steps.title: Use the exactly-one-intersection shortcut
steps.content:
The line and parabola intersect where their $y$-values are equal:
$$2x^2-19x+40=x+a$$
$$2x^2-20x+(40-a)=0$$
Exactly one intersection means this quadratic has one repeated solution, so its discriminant is $0$.

steps.title: Find the line and graph it
steps.content: Desmos gives $a=-10$. The actual line is therefore $y=x-10$, not $y=x+5$. Graphing $y=2x^2-19x+40$ and $y=x-10$ shows that the line just touches the parabola at one point.

steps.title: Confirm the x-value
steps.content:
Substitute $a=-10$ into the intersection equation:
$$2x^2-20x+50=0$$
Divide by $2$: $$x^2-10x+25=0$$
$$(x-5)^2=0$$
So the single intersection has $x=5$. That matches choice C, so C is correct.
